defs: A rib; a side; a region or coast.
quotes: Betwixt the costs of a ship.

records.ety: [OF. coster, couster, F. coter, fr. L. constare to stand at, to cost; con- + stare to stand. See Stand, and cf. Constant.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Cost; p. pr. & vb. n. Costing.]

defs: To require to be given, expended, or laid out therefor, as in barter, purchase, acquisition, etc.; to cause the cost, expenditure, relinquishment, or loss of; as, the ticket cost a dollar; the effort cost his life.
quotes: A diamond gone, cost me two thousand ducats.
Though it cost me ten nights' watchings.

defs: To require to be borne or suffered; to cause.
quotes: To do him wanton rites, which cost them woe.

records.ety: [OF. cost, F. cot. See Cost, v. t. ]

defs: The amount paid, charged, or engaged to be paid, for anything bought or taken in barter; charge; expense; hence, whatever, as labor, self-denial, suffering, etc., is requisite to secure benefit.
quotes: One day shall crown the alliance on 't so please you, Here at my house, and at my proper cost.
At less cost of life than is often expended in a skirmish, [Charles V.] saved Europe from invasion.

defs: Loss of any kind; detriment; pain; suffering.
quotes: I know thy trains, Though dearly to my cost, thy gins and toils.

notes: ☞ Costs in actions or suits are either between attorney and client, being what are payable in every case to the attorney or counsel by his client whether he ultimately succeed or not, or between party and party, being those which the law gives, or the court in its discretion decrees, to the prevailing, against the losing, party.
